strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Turns accepted parent-run handoff envelope evidence into a durable child TaskRecord while keeping child execution disabled.
Preserves Rust-owned task/run state boundaries so later scheduler work can attach child execution to existing runtime entities.

questions.prompt: Does this phase create a runtime entity instead of another blocked summary wrapper?
Does the child record preserve parent and source envelope provenance?
Can the same envelope fingerprint be processed without duplicate child tasks?
Does this phase avoid child LLM execution and external side effects?

answers.runtime_entity_created: Yes. An accepted handoff envelope materializes one child TaskRecord with TaskStatus::Queued.
answers.parent_child_provenance: Yes. The child stores parent_task_id, parent_run_id, source_candidate_id, source_handoff_envelope_id, and source_handoff_envelope_fingerprint.
answers.idempotency: Yes. find_child_task_by_handoff_fingerprint prevents duplicate child TaskRecord creation for the same parent run and envelope fingerprint.
answers.safety_boundary: Yes. The child is queued only; process execution, network, service control, workspace mutation, patch apply, scheduler handoff, and child LLM execution remain disabled.

exit_criteria: Accepted handoff envelope creates one child TaskRecord.
Child TaskRecord stores parent_task_id and parent_run_id.
Child TaskRecord stores source candidate ID and source handoff envelope fingerprint.
Same envelope fingerprint does not create a duplicate child TaskRecord.
No process exec, network, service control, patch apply, workspace mutation, scheduler handoff, or child LLM execution is added.
